steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says inefficient redox reactions bottleneck electron transfer in MFCs, limiting power. Adding silver more than doubles output. The researchers' hypothesis must explain how silver's conductivity overcomes that bottleneck.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — silver increases the number of electrons produced: The bottleneck is transfer efficiency, not electron quantity. More electrons don't help if transfer is still inefficient. ✗
  • Choice B — silver improves biofilm density first, then conductivity follows: Reverses the logical sequence; conductivity is the property silver is known for, not a downstream effect. ✗
  • Choice C — silver increases biofilm density: Density is not the stated bottleneck; inefficient redox reactions are. ✗
  • Choice D — silver provides a conductive path that bypasses inefficient redox reactions: Directly addresses the stated bottleneck by allowing electrons to transfer to the electrode without relying on slow redox steps. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
